# Blog Discoverability Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (if subagents available) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Add RSS feed, sitemap, robots.txt, Twitter cards, JSON-LD, and canonical URLs to the blog.

**Architecture:** Static files only — no build tools. RSS feed and sitemap are auto-generated by a Python script in a GitHub Action on every push to main. Meta tags are added inline to existing HTML files. Copilot instructions updated for future posts.

**Tech Stack:** Vanilla HTML, Python 3 (stdlib only), GitHub Actions

---

## File Structure

| File | Action | Responsibility |
|------|--------|----------------|
| `robots.txt` | Create | Allow all crawlers, point to sitemap |
| `index.html` | Modify | Add canonical, Twitter cards, JSON-LD WebSite, RSS autodiscovery |
| `engram.html` | Modify | Add canonical, Twitter cards, JSON-LD Article |
| `pediatric-disaster-edge-ai.html` | Modify | Add canonical, Twitter cards, JSON-LD Article |
| `distillation-rl-guardrails.html` | Modify | Add canonical, Twitter cards, JSON-LD Article |
| `governed-agent-distribution.html` | Modify | Add canonical, Twitter cards, JSON-LD Article |
| `autoresearch-harness-engineering.html` | Modify | Add canonical, Twitter cards, JSON-LD Article |
| `.github/scripts/update-featured.py` | Modify | Add feed.xml + sitemap.xml generation |
| `.github/workflows/update-featured.yml` | Modify | Commit feed.xml + sitemap.xml |
| `.github/copilot-instructions.md` | Modify | Add SEO requirements for future posts |
| `feed.xml` | Generated | RSS 2.0 feed |
| `sitemap.xml` | Generated | Sitemap protocol |

### Task 2: Add meta tags to index.html

**Files:** Modify `index.html` (head section, lines 3-11)

Insert after the `og:type` meta tag (line 10) and before the Mailchimp script (line 11).

- [ ] **Step 1: Add canonical, Twitter cards, RSS link, and JSON-LD to index.html**

Insert this block after `<meta property="og:type" content="website">` and before `<script id="mcjs">`:

```html
<link rel="canonical" href="https://ogkranthi.github.io/">
<link rel="alternate" type="application/rss+xml" title="Kranthi Manchikanti" href="https://ogkranthi.github.io/feed.xml">
<meta name="twitter:card" content="summary">
<meta name="twitter:site" content="@ogkranthi">
<meta name="twitter:title" content="Kranthi Manchikanti | AI Agents, Architectures & Applied Research">
<meta name="twitter:description" content="AI Agents, AI Architectures, Reinforcement Learning, HITL systems, and AI safety. Written from experience across high tech, finance and healthcare industries.">
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "WebSite",
  "name": "Kranthi Manchikanti",
  "url": "https://ogkranthi.github.io",
  "description": "Technical deep-dives on AI Agents, AI Architectures, Reinforcement Learning, HITL frameworks, and AI safety."
}
</script>
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Verify** — open index.html, confirm the new tags appear in `<head>` and the page still renders.

- [ ] **Step 3: Commit**

```bash
git add index.html
git commit -m "feat: add SEO meta tags, RSS link, and JSON-LD to index.html"
```

### Task 3: Add meta tags to all 5 post files

**Files:** Modify each of these (head section, after `og:type`, before Mailchimp script):
- `engram.html`

This task can be parallelized — each file is independent.

- [ ] **Step 1: Add meta tags to each post file**

For each post, insert after `<meta property="og:type" content="article">` and before `<script id="mcjs">`:

```html
<link rel="canonical" href="https://ogkranthi.github.io/{FILENAME}">
<meta name="twitter:card" content="summary">
<meta name="twitter:site" content="@ogkranthi">
<meta name="twitter:title" content="{VALUE FROM og:title}">
<meta name="twitter:description" content="{VALUE FROM og:description}">
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "Article",
  "headline": "{VALUE FROM og:title}",
  "description": "{VALUE FROM meta description}",
  "author": {
    "@type": "Person",
    "name": "Kranthi Manchikanti"
  },
  "publisher": {
    "@type": "Person",
    "name": "Kranthi Manchikanti"
  },
  "url": "https://ogkranthi.github.io/{FILENAME}"
}
</script>
```

**Per-file values:**

| File | og:title | og:description |
|------|----------|----------------|
| `engram.html` | Read from `<meta property="og:title">` | Read from `<meta property="og:description">` |
| `pediatric-disaster-edge-ai.html` | Read from `<meta property="og:title">` | Read from `<meta property="og:description">` |
| `distillation-rl-guardrails.html` | Read from `<meta property="og:title">` | Read from `<meta property="og:description">` |
| `governed-agent-distribution.html` | Read from `<meta property="og:title">` | Read from `<meta property="og:description">` |
| `autoresearch-harness-engineering.html` | Read from `<meta property="og:title">` | Read from `<meta property="og:description">` |

### Task 4: Update copilot-instructions.md

**Files:** Modify `.github/copilot-instructions.md`

- [ ] **Step 1: Add SEO requirements section**

Append after the existing "Publishing contract" section:

```markdown
## SEO requirements for every page

Every new blog post HTML must include these in `<head>`, after the `og:type` meta tag and before the Mailchimp script:

1. Canonical URL:
   `<link rel="canonical" href="https://ogkranthi.github.io/{filename}.html">`

2. Twitter card meta tags:
   ```html
   <meta name="twitter:card" content="summary">
   <meta name="twitter:site" content="@ogkranthi">
   <meta name="twitter:title" content="{same as og:title}">
   <meta name="twitter:description" content="{same as og:description}">
   ```

3. JSON-LD Article structured data:
   ```html
   <script type="application/ld+json">
   {
     "@context": "https://schema.org",
     "@type": "Article",
     "headline": "{article title}",
     "description": "{meta description}",
     "author": { "@type": "Person", "name": "Kranthi Manchikanti" },
     "publisher": { "@type": "Person", "name": "Kranthi Manchikanti" },
     "url": "https://ogkranthi.github.io/{filename}.html"
   }
   </script>
   ```

These are required. Do not publish a post without them.
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Commit**

```bash
git add .github/copilot-instructions.md
git commit -m "feat: add SEO meta tag requirements to copilot instructions"
```

## Chunk 2: Automation — RSS Feed and Sitemap Generation

### Task 5: Extend update-featured.py with feed.xml generation

**Files:** Modify `.github/scripts/update-featured.py`

- [ ] **Step 1: Add RSS generation function**

Add a `generate_feed` function that:
1. Parses all post cards from index.html (reuse the existing card regex, but find ALL matches not just first)
2. Skips cards with `href="#"`
3. Builds RSS 2.0 XML with channel metadata and one `<item>` per real post
4. Date format: parse "Mar 2026" → "01 Mar 2026 00:00:00 GMT" (RFC 822)
5. Writes to `feed.xml`

```python
import datetime

SITE_URL = "https://ogkranthi.github.io"

MONTH_MAP = {
    "Jan": 1, "Feb": 2, "Mar": 3, "Apr": 4, "May": 5, "Jun": 6,
    "Jul": 7, "Aug": 8, "Sep": 9, "Oct": 10, "Nov": 11, "Dec": 12,
}


def parse_post_date(date_line):
    """Parse 'Mar 2026 · 15 min' into an RFC 822 date string."""
    m = re.match(r"(\w+)\s+(\d{4})", date_line)
    if not m:
        return ""
    month_str, year = m.group(1), int(m.group(2))
    month = MONTH_MAP.get(month_str, 1)
    dt = datetime.datetime(year, month, 1, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)
    return dt.strftime("%a, %d %b %Y %H:%M:%S +0000")


def extract_all_cards(html):
    """Extract all post cards from index.html."""
    pattern = re.compile(
        r'<article\s+class="post-card[^"]*"[^>]*'
        r"onclick=\"window\.location\.href='([^']+)'\""
        r'[^>]*>'
        r'.*?<h3>(.*?)</h3>'
        r'.*?<p\s+class="post-excerpt">(.*?)</p>'
        r'.*?<span\s+class="post-date">(.*?)</span>'
        r'.*?</article>',
        re.DOTALL,
    )
    cards = []
    for m in pattern.finditer(html):
        href = m.group(1).strip()
        if href == "#":
            continue
        cards.append({
            "href": href,
            "title": m.group(2).strip(),
            "excerpt": m.group(3).strip(),
            "date_line": m.group(4).strip(),
        })
    return cards


def generate_feed(html):
    """Generate feed.xml from post cards in index.html."""
    cards = extract_all_cards(html)
    if not cards:
        print("No real post cards found, skipping feed generation")
        return

    items = []
    for card in cards:
        pub_date = parse_post_date(card["date_line"])
        date_tag = f"\n      <pubDate>{pub_date}</pubDate>" if pub_date else ""
        items.append(
            f"    <item>\n"
            f"      <title>{escape_xml(card['title'])}</title>\n"
            f"      <link>{SITE_URL}/{card['href']}</link>\n"
            f"      <guid>{SITE_URL}/{card['href']}</guid>\n"
            f"      <description>{escape_xml(card['excerpt'])}</description>{date_tag}\n"
            f"    </item>"
        )

    feed = (
        '<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>\n'
        '<rss version="2.0" xmlns:atom="http://www.w3.org/2005/Atom">\n'
        "  <channel>\n"
        "    <title>Kranthi Manchikanti</title>\n"
        f"    <link>{SITE_URL}</link>\n"
        f'    <atom:link href="{SITE_URL}/feed.xml" rel="self" type="application/rss+xml"/>\n'
        "    <description>Technical deep-dives on AI Agents, AI Architectures, Reinforcement Learning, HITL frameworks, and AI safety.</description>\n"
        "    <language>en-us</language>\n"
        + "\n".join(items) + "\n"
        "  </channel>\n"
        "</rss>\n"
    )

    Path("feed.xml").write_text(feed, encoding="utf-8")
    print(f"feed.xml generated with {len(cards)} items")


def escape_xml(text):
    """Escape XML special characters."""
    return text.replace("&", "&amp;").replace("<", "&lt;").replace(">", "&gt;").replace('"', "&quot;")
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Verify** — run script, check feed.xml is valid XML with correct items.

```bash
python3 .github/scripts/update-featured.py
cat feed.xml
```

---

### Task 6: Add sitemap.xml generation to update-featured.py

**Files:** Modify `.github/scripts/update-featured.py`

- [ ] **Step 1: Add sitemap generation function**

```python
import os


def generate_sitemap():
    """Generate sitemap.xml from HTML files on disk."""
    pages = ["index.html"]
    for f in sorted(Path(".").glob("*.html")):
        if f.name != "index.html":
            pages.append(f.name)

    entries = []
    for page in pages:
        path = Path(page)
        if not path.exists():
            continue
        mtime = datetime.datetime.fromtimestamp(
            os.path.getmtime(path), tz=datetime.timezone.utc
        )
        lastmod = mtime.strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
        entries.append(
            f"  <url>\n"
            f"    <loc>{SITE_URL}/{page}</loc>\n"
            f"    <lastmod>{lastmod}</lastmod>\n"
            f"  </url>"
        )

    sitemap = (
        '<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>\n'
        '<urlset xmlns="http://www.sitemaps.org/schemas/sitemap/0.9">\n'
        + "\n".join(entries) + "\n"
        "</urlset>\n"
    )

    Path("sitemap.xml").write_text(sitemap, encoding="utf-8")
    print(f"sitemap.xml generated with {len(entries)} URLs")
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Call both generators from main()**

At the end of the `main()` function, after the existing featured/count logic, add:

```python
    # ── Generate feed and sitemap ──
    generate_feed(html)
    generate_sitemap()
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Verify** — run script, check both files.

```bash
python3 .github/scripts/update-featured.py
cat feed.xml
cat sitemap.xml
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git add .github/scripts/update-featured.py
git commit -m "feat: extend update script to generate feed.xml and sitemap.xml"
```

### Task 7: Update workflow to commit generated files

**Files:** Modify `.github/workflows/update-featured.yml`

- [ ] **Step 1: Update the commit step** to include feed.xml and sitemap.xml

Change:
```yaml
      - name: Commit if changed
        run: |
          git diff --quiet index.html && exit 0
          git config user.name  "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
          git add index.html
          git commit -m "chore: auto-update featured post and post count"
          git push
```

To:
```yaml
      - name: Commit if changed
        run: |
          git config user.name  "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
          git add index.html feed.xml sitemap.xml
          git diff --cached --quiet && exit 0
          git commit -m "chore: auto-update featured post, feed, and sitemap"
          git push
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Commit**

```bash
git add .github/workflows/update-featured.yml
git commit -m "feat: include feed.xml and sitemap.xml in auto-update workflow"
```
